sections.heading: Definition of Company
sections.body_text: Defines a 'company' as a company incorporated under the Companies Act, 2013 or under any previous company law.
sections.chapter: I - Preliminary

sections.heading: Formation of Company
sections.body_text: Specifies the requirements for forming a company, including the minimum number of members for private, public, and one-person companies.
sections.chapter: II - Incorporation of Company and Matters Incidental Thereto

sections.heading: Memorandum of Association
sections.body_text: Details the requirements for the memorandum of association, including its contents and the procedure for alteration.
sections.chapter: II - Incorporation of Company and Matters Incidental Thereto

sections.heading: Articles of Association
sections.body_text: Specifies the contents of the articles of association and the procedure for their alteration.
sections.chapter: II - Incorporation of Company and Matters Incidental Thereto

sections.heading: Corporate Social Responsibility
sections.body_text: Mandates certain companies to constitute a Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) Committee and spend at least 2% of their average net profits on CSR activities.
sections.chapter: IX - Accounts of Companies

sections.heading: Related Party Transactions
sections.body_text: Regulates transactions between a company and its related parties, requiring board or shareholder approval for specified transactions.
sections.chapter: XII - Meetings of Board and its Powers

sections.heading: Application to Tribunal for Relief in Cases of Oppression, etc.
sections.body_text: Allows members to apply to the Tribunal for relief if the company's affairs are being conducted in a manner prejudicial to public interest or oppressive to any member.
sections.chapter: XVI - Prevention of Oppression and Mismanagement

sections.heading: Powers of Tribunal
sections.body_text: Empowers the Tribunal to pass orders to regulate the conduct of the company's affairs, including the removal of directors or alteration of the memorandum or articles.
sections.chapter: XVI - Prevention of Oppression and Mismanagement

sections.heading: Consequences of Termination or Modification of Certain Agreements
sections.body_text: Specifies the consequences of orders passed by the Tribunal under Section 242, including the termination or modification of agreements.
sections.chapter: XVI - Prevention of Oppression and Mismanagement

sections.heading: Right to Apply under Section 241
sections.body_text: Specifies the eligibility criteria for members to apply to the Tribunal under Section 241, including the minimum shareholding or membership requirements.
sections.chapter: XVI - Prevention of Oppression and Mismanagement

sections.heading: Class Action
sections.body_text: Empowers members or depositors to file a class action suit against the company, its directors, or auditors for any fraudulent or wrongful act.
sections.chapter: XVI - Prevention of Oppression and Mismanagement

sections.heading: Application of Certain Provisions to Proceedings under Section 241 or Section 245
sections.body_text: Specifies the application of certain provisions of the Code of Civil Procedure to proceedings under Sections 241 and 245.
sections.chapter: XVI - Prevention of Oppression and Mismanagement

sections.heading: Circumstances in which Company may be Wound Up by Tribunal
sections.body_text: Lists the circumstances under which a company may be wound up by the Tribunal, including inability to pay debts or acting against public interest.
sections.chapter: XX - Winding Up

sections.heading: Petition for Winding Up
sections.body_text: Specifies who may file a petition for winding up of a company, including the company itself, creditors, or the Registrar.
sections.chapter: XX - Winding Up

sections.heading: Punishment for Fraud
sections.body_text: Prescribes punishment for fraud, including imprisonment for a term which may extend to 10 years and a fine not less than the amount involved in the fraud.
sections.chapter: XXIX - Miscellaneous

sections.heading: Punishment for False Statements
sections.body_text: Provides for punishment for making false statements in any return, report, certificate, or other document required under the Act.
sections.chapter: XXIX - Miscellaneous

sections.heading: Punishment for False Evidence
sections.body_text: Prescribes punishment for giving false evidence in any investigation or inquiry under the Act, including imprisonment for a term which may extend to 7 years.
sections.chapter: XXIX - Miscellaneous

sections.heading: Investigation into Affairs of Company by Serious Fraud Investigation Office
sections.body_text: Empowers the central government to assign the investigation of a company to the Serious Fraud Investigation Office (SFIO) in cases of fraud.
sections.chapter: XIV - Inspection, Inquiry and Investigation

sections.heading: Company to have Board of Directors
sections.body_text: Mandates every company to have a board of directors, specifying the minimum and maximum number of directors and the requirement for independent directors.
sections.chapter: XI - Appointment and Qualifications of Directors

sections.heading: Duties of Directors
sections.body_text: Specifies the fiduciary duties of directors, including acting in good faith, avoiding conflicts of interest, and exercising due diligence.
sections.chapter: XI - Appointment and Qualifications of Directors

sections.heading: Investigation into Company’s Affairs in Other Cases
sections.body_text: Empowers the Tribunal to order an investigation into the affairs of a company if it is in the public interest or if there is evidence of fraud or mismanagement.
sections.chapter: XIV - Inspection, Inquiry and Investigation

sections.heading: Appeal from Orders of Tribunal
sections.body_text: Provides for appeals to the National Company Law Appellate Tribunal (NCLAT) against orders of the National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT).
sections.chapter: XXVII - National Company Law Tribunal and Appellate Tribunal

sections.heading: Punishment for Wrongful Withholding of Property
sections.body_text: Prescribes punishment for any officer or employee of a company who wrongfully withholds property of the company, including imprisonment and fines.
sections.chapter: XXIX - Miscellaneous
